plugins.name: second-opinion
plugins.description: Get 1-3 independent second opinions from fresh Claude reviewers to strengthen your reasoning before presenting to the user.

plugins.name: preflight
plugins.description: Honest self-assessment skill — surfaces incomplete work, missing verifications, bugs, and improvement ideas before shipping.

plugins.name: verify-result
plugins.description: Black-box verification of any agent output — code, documents, presentations, reports, configs. Examine from the consumer's perspective and show evidence.

plugins.name: verify-claims
plugins.description: Identify and verify unverified claims in the agent's own responses before presenting them as conclusions.

plugins.name: pre-mortem
plugins.description: Systematically identify how a plan or design could fail before committing to it.

plugins.name: design-options
plugins.description: Generate multiple strong design options with explicit tradeoff profiles, starting from the ideal architecture.

plugins.name: steel-man-own-position
plugins.description: Before flipping a previously-stated position under pressure, restate its strongest form and the goal it served, then check whether the trigger refutes the core on the same goal — not just a decorative argument or a different axis.

plugins.name: align
plugins.description: Surface what I think we're doing before acting on it — produces a structured manifest exposing every silent commitment so the user can correct course before any artifact is produced.

plugins.name: silent-failures
plugins.description: Audit error handling for silent failures, inadequate feedback, and inappropriate fallbacks — delegates to the silent-failure-hunter subagent for isolation

plugins.name: explore
plugins.description: Map the terrain before designing — locate, trace, pattern, constrain. Used before any substantive design or planning step. Output is mostly for the agent's grounding; only surprises and hard constraints surface to the user.

plugins.name: clarify
plugins.description: Surface and resolve underspecified decisions before designing. Asks targeted questions about scope, edge behavior, trade-offs, integration, constraints, and audience — with defaults — so design choices aren't silently locked around hidden assumptions.

plugins.name: reflect
plugins.description: Capture durable lessons from a session before they fade — what surprised you, what friction repeated, what should update CLAUDE.md or notes. Output goes to memory, notes, or global instructions, not just to chat.

plugins.name: handoff
plugins.description: Write a durable handoff for work that's about to ship — structured PR descriptions, summaries, memos, or memory notes. Tells the next reader what was built, why, what changed, and what's next, without forcing them to spelunk the diff.

plugins.name: spec
plugins.description: Write the implementation spec — context, approach, files to modify, utilities to reuse, verification — that drives execution after design is chosen.

plugins.name: understudy
plugins.description: Write code, comments, tests, or commit messages that read as if the project's own maintainer wrote them, so a contribution is accepted as native rather than rejected as foreign. A foreign-looking patch is a cost to the maintainer even when it's correct.

plugins.name: worktrunk-hook
plugins.description: Route Claude Code's auto-created git worktrees through worktrunk so background and --worktree sessions inherit project hooks (env symlinks, installs, port allocation).

plugins.name: drive-browser
plugins.description: Drive a browser with Playwright as a dual-mode superset — resilient locators for debugging your own web app and a vision/coordinate loop for opaque real sites, on a fresh browser or the user's real logged-in session.
